task_completion.description: Draw a 5-sided polygon (pentagon) in the sketch.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A closed 2D sketch consisting of exactly 5 straight line segments is visible in the SolveSpace canvas.

task_completion.description: Constrain the pentagon's side length to 25 mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dimension constraint showing '25' or '25.00' is attached to at least one of the pentagon's sides.

task_completion.description: Constrain the pentagon to be a regular shape (equal sides and angles).
task_completion.visual_evidence: Angle constraints showing '108' or '108.00' degrees between adjacent sides, or equal length/angle constraint symbols applied to the sides, ensuring it is a regular pentagon.

task_completion.description: Ensure the sketch is fully constrained.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace property browser (usually top left text area) displays '0 degrees of freedom' or 'ok', and/or the sketch lines have turned green indicating they are fully constrained.

task_completion.description: Extrude the pentagon into a 3D prism with a depth of 15 mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D pentagonal prism is visible on the canvas, and the extrusion depth property shows '15' or '15.00' in the property browser or as a dimension.

task_completion.description: Save the 3D model to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog shows the path '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/' and filename 'pentagon_prism.slvs', or the SolveSpace window title bar displays 'pentagon_prism.slvs'.

integrity.description: The agent used the SolveSpace graphical user interface to create the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the SolveSpace application window, menus, and toolbars, rather than terminal commands generating the file.

integrity.description: The model was built progressively from a blank canvas.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the step-by-step process of drawing lines, applying constraints, and extruding, rather than just opening a pre-existing completed file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a text editor to manually write the .slvs file.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, or gedit) are shown editing a .slvs file directly to bypass the CAD modeling process.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file path is '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/pentagon_prism.slvs'. The regular pentagon must have a side length of exactly 25 mm and internal angles of exactly 108°. The extrusion depth for the prism must be exactly 15 mm. A fully constrained sketch in SolveSpace typically shows '0 degrees of freedom' in the property browser.
